title: EU AI Act Compliance Control
description: Schema for controls in the eu-ai-act-blueprint controls catalog. Each control represents a technical or organizational measure mapped to EU AI Act requirements and cross-referenced with major AI security and governance frameworks.

control_id.description: Unique identifier. Format: AISEC-{DOMAIN}-{SEQ}. Domains: RM (Risk Management), DG (Data Governance), TD (Technical Documentation), LG (Logging), TR (Transparency), HO (Human Oversight), AC (Accuracy), RB (Robustness), CS (Cybersecurity), PM (Post-Market Monitoring), CA (Conformity Assessment), DP (Deployer), GP (GPAI), PH (Prohibited), GV (Governance).

control_name.description: Short, descriptive name for the control.

description.description: Full description of the control objective, scope, and expected implementation.

ai_act_article.description: EU AI Act article(s) this control addresses. Format: 'Art. X(Y)' with semicolons between multiple articles.

ai_act_obligation.description: Whether the control is legally mandatory under the AI Act, recommended as best practice, or addresses a prohibited practice.

actor_role.description: Which AI Act actor(s) this control applies to. Values: 'Provider', 'Deployer', 'Provider; Deployer'.

risk_category.description: AI Act risk tier(s) this control applies to.

nist_ai_rmf.description: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI 100-1) function and subcategory. Format: 'FUNCTION X.Y'. Functions: GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE.

iso_42001.description: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 clause(s) and Annex A controls. Format: 'X.Y.Z' for clauses, 'A.N' for Annex A controls.

owasp_llm_top10.description: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (2025) risk(s) addressed. Format: 'LLMNN:Name'. Use '—' if not applicable.

mitre_atlas.description: MITRE ATLAS technique(s) mitigated. Format: 'AML.TNNNN'. Use '—' if not applicable.

gdpr_article.description: GDPR (Regulation 2016/679) article(s) where requirements overlap or reinforce. Use '—' if not applicable.

dora_article.description: DORA (Regulation 2022/2554) article(s) where requirements overlap or reinforce. Use '—' if not applicable.

implementation_evidence.description: Relative path to the code, configuration, or document in the project/ directory that implements this control.

status.description: Implementation status of this control in the project. planned = roadmap commitment; documented = specification exists in docs/ or as code stub; implemented = working code exists; tested = automated tests pass.

maturity_level.description: Implementation maturity for this control. 1 = Documented (specification exists in writing); 2 = Implemented (working code/process exists); 3 = Tested (automated tests verify the implementation). Collapsed from the original CMMI-style 5-level scale to match what this project can honestly demonstrate.

phase.description: Build phase when this control should be implemented. Phase 1=Foundation, 2=Data, 3=Documentation, 4=Core Engineering, 5=Compliance, 6=Polish.
